title: Pick & Play | con Thea e Kayleigh: chi pesca la domanda più difficile?
description: Pesca la domanda e rispondi! Protagoniste di questo episodio, le rossonere Thea Kyvåg e Kayleigh van Dooren ⚫ Acquista ...

comments.author: @otrebor9170
comments.text:
Da tifoso non accetto la mediocrità e non la accetterò mai passivamente. Il Milan non può essere ridotto a “soluzione meno peggio”, a minestrina, a quarto posto come orizzonte massimo, a progetto da raccontare ogni estate dopo aver distrutto quello precedente.

Però ormai la sensazione è che ci stiano prendendo per sfinimento. Tre settimane di nulla, casting infiniti, nomi buttati lì, incontri, attese, deadline spostate. Alla fine arrivi quasi a dire: va bene, fate almeno qualcosa. Ed è questo il punto più triste.

Rangnick-Glasner può anche essere meglio del vuoto totale, ma non deve diventare l’alibi per assolvere chi ha portato il Milan a questo livello.

Io spero solo che questa proprietà se ne vada prima di fare altri danni. Perché ogni errore in più, ogni giocatore svalutato, ogni talento perso, ogni stagione buttata, rallenterà anche il percorso di crescita di un’eventuale nuova società.

Il problema non è solo oggi. È il Milan che rischiamo di ritrovarci domani, pieno di macerie da ricostruire.

comments.author: @JTRave
comments.text:
Biggest 3 moments for me:

1) Cape Verde 0-0 Spain. Biggest upset of the tournament that’s already had a lot of shocks; for the odds-on favorite to fail to win against a debutant nation with a population of 500k is an incredible achievement for the underdogs. Their World Cup is, frankly, already a success and everything from here is just icing.

2) USA 4-1 Paraguay. Yes, perhaps a little bit of bias, but it must be understood that this isn’t just a strong win. It’s literally Team USA’s greatest game of football they’ve ever played in terms of skill. For many Americans, we’ve been promised our whole lives that the USA was always on the precipice of reaching “the next level”, but then we’re still losing in the same spots, and even when we win it’s by slim margins and on the backs of scrappy play. This was different. This was Team USA being far and away the “better team” in a way that we typically only see from the best European and South American sides. So in that sense, this match was truly historic. It’s an indication that this “Golden Generation” may not be squandered, and there is legitimate hope for a deep run.

3) Mbappe and Messi. Last tournament’s superstar finalists. Each has a World Cup already to his name. One of them is the greatest player in the world. The other is the greatest player of all time. And they both showed up Matchday 1 and BROUGHT IT. Mbappe with two goals against a quality Senegal side, his second goal seemed like it was out of pure spite. Messi with a hat trick that shows the full diversity of his skillset at 38 years old. They both seem destined to not just break but shatter Klose’s career World Cup goals record. And it is so nice to see these stars rise to the occasion and prove to the world that even at the very top level, there’s a difference between “world class” and something greater.

comments.author: @FreddysCat
comments.text:
Just wanted to see a competitive match, but it was more Coppa di Merda. English announcers sounding more and more like the UK's hyperbolic American masters. ... Every player name preceded by the name of the foreign colony ("club") he plays for in the UK. ... 2 goals in. Bored. Back to Verona. More authenticity and insight into the sport and life generally--for better and worse--on one page than anything that will happen in El Norte. Infantino guastafeste.

'A journalist called me up from Gazzetta dello Sport to say he was preparing a book on the great teams of the last thirty years and had I followed Hellas Verona in the miraculous 1985. I had to admit, not without an old chagrin, that I hadn’t. I hadn’t had a son as an excuse at the time. I couldn’t plead family matters and male bonding.

'To avoid the memory of one of life’s great missed opportunities, I ask him, ‘What do you think of Trapattoni’s Italy?’ The national team has a new coach. ‘Couldn’t care less,’ the sports journalist says. ‘I’m a Genoa fan.’ '

No reason for a Christian to value someone more because a grandparent (FIFA's standard) came from a particular place on the map. To care about nations seems hidebound anyway as they become more and more abstracted. (This is why the right are so rabidly insisting on them today. And, of course, mine is better than yours.) Globalism began taking shape during the Age of Exploration 500 years ago, so don't go on too much about your latest conspiracy while at the same time buying oranges during the bleak mid-winter.

club > country

(Notifications off)

comments.text:
Uruguay contributed to their own downfall but Cape Verde were no slouches, they went forward, had some amazing turns and flick passes. The turn and run that led to the free kick goal was outstanding. The 2nd goal clearly a mistake by Muslera coming off his line, but it was still amazing skill from cape verde one touch off his thigh and finish with the other boot. It was a lot of fun to watch.

In fact two of the most fun group stage games have been 2-2 draws, this match and holland vs. japan.
